title: calophyllum havilandii
intro: Calophyllum havilandii, also known as Burmese canarium or Malayan canarium, is a species of evergreen tree native to Southeast Asia, particularly in Malaysia and Indonesia. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of calophyllic acid and other bioactive compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits associated with Calophyllum havilandii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Calophyllum havilandii is often used in herbal preparations, such as tinctures, extracts, and oils, which can be applied topically or consumed as dietary supplements to harness its medicinal properties.
